teaching_point: An object at rest will remain at rest unless a force causes it to move.
prompt: A toy car sits still on a smooth, wooden floor. A hand gently pushes it, and it rolls forward a short distance. The background is a brightly lit playroom.

criteria.1: Depict a toy car as the primary object throughout the video
criteria.2: Show a human hand making visible physical contact with the toy car
criteria.3: Display the toy car and hand on a visibly smooth wooden floor for the entire duration
criteria.4: Present the setting as a brightly lit playroom in all frames
criteria.5: Sequence the actions so the car is stationary, then pushed by the hand, then moves forward

criteria.1: Show the toy car completely stationary before the hand makes contact
criteria.2: Depict the hand making contact and applying force to the car before any car movement begins
criteria.3: Show the car starting to move only after the hand pushes it
criteria.4: Depict the car rolling forward for a short distance after being pushed and then coming to a complete stop
criteria.5: Avoid any reversal, skipping, or time jumps in the sequence of actions

criteria.1: Show the car remaining stationary until the hand applies force
criteria.2: Depict the car moving only while the hand is in contact or immediately after contact ends
criteria.3: Show the car rolling forward smoothly, with no unexplained changes in speed or direction
criteria.4: Depict the car gradually slowing down after the push, consistent with friction on a smooth wooden floor
criteria.5: Show the wheels rotating in sync with the car's forward motion, with no sliding or floating
criteria.6: Prevent any visible penetration, levitation, or physically impossible interactions between the hand, car, and floor

criteria.1: Demonstrate the hand pushing the toy car as the clear cause of the car's forward motion
criteria.2: Show the car rolling forward a short, observable distance after being pushed
criteria.3: Depict the scenario in a way that visually demonstrates cause (push) and effect (car moves)
criteria.4: Avoid any motion of the car that is not directly caused by the hand's push

criteria.1: Maintain consistent lighting throughout the entire video
criteria.2: Keep the appearance and color of the toy car, hand, and floor unchanged from start to finish
criteria.3: Ensure the car's movement is continuous, with no visual jumps or discontinuities
criteria.4: Prevent any sudden changes in the background or setting during the video

criteria.1: Exclude any objects other than the toy car, hand, and floor from the main action area
criteria.2: Avoid visual effects or animations that could suggest non-physical causes for the car's motion
criteria.3: Prevent any actions or events that contradict the teaching point, such as the car moving without being pushed
criteria.4: Omit distracting audio or visual cues unrelated to the scenario
